title: Cells, Walls, Passages, Boundaries, and Invariants · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reciprocal wall and passage model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy.
Carve a passage as one atomic reciprocal update between adjacent cells.
Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug puzzle team handles case 2188 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Topology Validation
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-topology-validation-12s788" that uses case 3740 to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Topology Validation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Topology Validation into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Topology Validation, keep Topology Validation connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-topology-validation-12s788
# Competency: maze-topology-validation.
# Case 3740: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_topology_validation_12s788(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_12s788 = "12s788-887s21-3740"
    assert evidence_variant_12s788
    evidence_axes_12s788 = (
        "grid12s788",
        "topology3740",
        "search887s21",
        "event12s7883740",
        "access374012s788",
        "recovery887s213740",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_12s788)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.instruction:
Order the Topology Validation Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python player-to-native-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Topology Validation.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Topology Validation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Topology Validation, keep Topology Validation connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug puzzle team handles case 7759 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; all graph claims need an independent witness; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Reconcile cell count, neighbor pairs, reciprocal openings, boundary exceptions, and illegal diagonal links.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Topology Validation has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Publish the incident record · Debug Topology Validation

Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reconcile cell count, neighbor pairs, reciprocal openings, boundary exceptions, and illegal diagonal links.
